steps.title: Identify the punctuation rule being tested
steps.content: The blank completes "chemistry ______ and his colleagues at Temple University recently developed..." The structure requires a title + name phrase to serve as the first element of a compound subject joined by "and." The SAT rule: no comma is needed between a title and the proper name it directly precedes, and no comma is needed after the name when it continues into a compound subject.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — professor Yugang Sun: no comma between title and name, no comma after the name — both correct, producing the clean compound subject "chemistry professor Yugang Sun and his colleagues." ✓
  • Choice B — professor Yugang Sun,: the comma after "Sun" incorrectly separates the first element of the compound subject from the coordinating conjunction "and" — this is an unnecessary comma error. ✗
  • Choice C — professor, Yugang Sun,: the comma before "Yugang Sun" incorrectly separates the title from the name, and the comma after "Sun" incorrectly interrupts the compound subject — double unnecessary comma error. ✗
  • Choice D — professor, Yugang Sun: the comma between "professor" and "Yugang Sun" incorrectly separates the title from the proper name it modifies — this is an unnecessary comma error. ✗
A is correct.
